details: Begin numerical stability analysis for backward_softmax
Listed ttnn/ttnn/operations/backward_softmax/** to discover entry points
Read backward_softmax.py — confirmed float32-only, tile-aligned validation, no compute_kernel_config exposed

Read backward_softmax_program_descriptor.py — confirmed math_fidelity=HiFi4, fp32_dest_acc_en=True, all intermediate CBs (cb_prod, cb_sum, cb_centered) are Float32, cb_scaler is bfloat16 (1.0), no UnpackToDestMode set
Read backward_softmax_compute.cpp — two-pass: (mul + accumulate_reduce_block) then (sub<SCALAR> + mul); identified catastrophic cancellation site at sub<SCALAR>(dy, s)
Read backward_softmax_reader.cpp — scaler prepared once via calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler<SUM,...> (factor=1.0, no divide-then-sum); per-lane lockstep dual-tensor streaming
Read backward_softmax_writer.cpp — straight per-tile drain to DRAM; no precision events
Read streaming_reduce_helpers.hpp/.inl: accumulate_reduce_block uses Accumulate::at(cb_acc, b) for inter-block reload (round-trip via cb_sum)
Confirmed scaler factor=SUM_AND_MAX_REDUCE_FACTOR=1 (sum-only reduce, no 1/N scaler)
Severity assessment: cancellation at (dy - s) is the dominant precision risk; fp32 dest is the only mitigation; algorithmic form has no cancellation-free O(N) alternative
Identified gap: UnpackToDestMode::UnpackToDestFp32 is not set on cb_sum despite Float32 CB + fp32 dest — inter-block reload may go through TF32 SrcA path
Noted Wormhole B0 hardware bug #38306: HiFi4 + fp32_dest_acc_en is reportedly unsafe; op uses both with no caller override
